seoTitle: How to Set an Email Alias in Outlook (2026)
metaDescription: Create and configure an additional Outlook email alias for a business brand or campaign, then route incoming mail into dedicated folders automatically.
intro: Running separate brands or campaigns doesn't have to mean juggling multiple logins. This guide covers creating a new Outlook alias, switching your default sign-in identity, sending mail from the new address, and sorting incoming alias mail into its own folder.

steps.heading: Open your Microsoft account settings
steps.body: Go to account.microsoft.com in your browser and sign into your primary profile.

steps.heading: Access your info
steps.body: Click the "Your info" tab and scroll down to find "Manage how you sign in to Microsoft."

steps.heading: Verify your identity
steps.body: Complete any required security check to unlock the account alias settings.

steps.heading: Add a new alias
steps.body: Click "Add email" under the Account Alias header, type your desired address, and confirm to add it.

steps.heading: Send from your new alias
steps.body: In Outlook, open a new message, click the hidden From dropdown, and select your new alias to send from that identity.

steps.heading: Sort incoming alias mail
steps.body: Set up a mailbox rule so mail sent to your alias lands in a dedicated folder automatically.

faq.question: Do I need a separate password for my alias?
faq.answer: No, aliases route into the same account, so you continue signing in with your existing primary credentials.

faq.question: Can I make my alias my default sign-in identity?
faq.answer: Yes, you can click "Make primary" next to any verified secondary alias to change your default login identity.

faq.question: Will replies to my alias come back to the same inbox?
faq.answer: Yes, since aliases route into your single unified mailbox, you can use a rule to sort those replies into a dedicated folder.

faq.question: How many aliases can I add to one account?
faq.answer: Microsoft allows a limited number of aliases per account, so check your account settings for the current cap.
